mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand, which directly translates to higher corporate revenues and earnings, thereby driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending is a major component of GDP and corporate revenue
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns for S&P 500 consistently show a negative or statistically insignificant correlation with RSAFS month-over-month growth over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below a critical threshold signals a tightening labor market, which typically precedes wage inflation and higher consumer spending, boosting equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 5.0% for two consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:Low unemployment often correlates with economic expansion and higher wages
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0% over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is rising rapidly while the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) remains exceptionally low, it indicates an accommodative monetary policy relative to inflation, which can fuel asset price appreciation due to negative real interest rates, boosting S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Negative real rates encourage risk-taking and asset speculation
failure_condition: If S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 60% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong industrial production growth (INDPRO), a robust supply side supports overall economic expansion, leading to higher corporate profits and thus positive S&P 500 returns. This effect is particularly pronounced during post-recession recovery phases over a 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a key indicator of manufacturing health and economic output
failure_condition: If the correlation between INDPRO growth and S&P 500 20-day returns is negative or statistically insignificant (p > 0.1) during identified post-recession recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained decline in housing starts (HOUST) indicates a weakening in the construction sector and broader economic demand, signaling potential future economic contraction and negative S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1000.0 (thousands of units) for two consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts are a leading economic indicator of future economic activity
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where HOUST drops below the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a healthy and expanding labor market, which supports consumer confidence and spending, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Job growth is fundamental to economic health and consumer purchasing power
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward returns for S&P 500 consistently show a negative or statistically insignificant correlation with PAYEMS month-over-month growth over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is rising significantly while inflation (CPIAUCSL) is also high, it signals aggressive monetary tightening to combat inflation, which typically dampens economic activity and equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by > 0.25% in 3 months AND CPIAUCSL 12-month change > 3%
evidence_refs: fact:Aggressive monetary tightening can lead to economic slowdowns or recessions
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 30% of triggered instances during periods of aggressive monetary tightening.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic contraction, a decline in GDP (GDPC1) signals a broad-based weakening of demand, leading to lower corporate earnings and negative S&P 500 returns. This effect is particularly strong during officially declared recessions over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth is negative for two consecutive quarters
evidence_refs: fact:GDP contraction is a hallmark of economic recession
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances during officially declared recession periods where GDPC1 shows contraction.
